description_long: This bungalow features a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof with brown shingle tiling and a wide overhang. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray material, likely stucco or concrete, and the front facade is lined with a series of large, evenly spaced rectangular windows. The entrance is sheltered by a deep porch with a triangular pediment, supported by solid side walls. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and a continuation of the roof's overhang. The structure sits on a flat, paved base, and the overall design is compact and single-storied. The architectural style is reminiscent of a modernized Craftsman bungalow, emphasizing clean lines and functional form.
description: A single-story bungalow with a steep gable roof, wide overhangs, and a front porch, finished in light gray with large front windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide gable roof, large front windows, and the sheltered porch area.
description_view_1: Oblique front-left view highlighting the triangular pediment, porch entrance, and side wall details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the extended roof overhang and minimal window openings on the back facade.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the length of the roof and the simple, windowless side wall.
